meta.certificationName: Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer
meta.title: GCP-MLE-6: Vertex AI Production ML, Pipelines, Monitoring, and Responsible AI

domains.name: Monitoring, Optimizing, and Maintaining ML Solutions

questions.stem:
A product analyst has a clean tabular dataset and needs a baseline classification model quickly with minimal custom code.

Which Vertex AI approach should the ML engineer start with?

options.text: Write a custom distributed PyTorch training loop first
Deploy the raw BigQuery table directly to an endpoint
Use Vertex AI AutoML for tabular classification
Configure Model Monitoring before any model exists

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).

**C** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI AutoML overview](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/beginner/beginners-guide).

questions.stem:
A research team needs a custom TensorFlow training loop with a private container image and GPU accelerators.

Which Vertex AI capability fits this requirement?

options.text: Use BigQuery ML only with SQL statements
Create a Feature Store online serving node
Send training examples to an endpoint prediction route
Run a Vertex AI custom training job with the custom container

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI custom training](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/overview).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI custom training](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/overview).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI custom training](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/overview).

**D** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Vertex AI custom training](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/training/overview).

questions.stem:
A fraud model must return predictions to a payment API in under 200 ms.

Which serving pattern should be used?

options.text: Deploy the model to a Vertex AI endpoint for online prediction
Run a nightly batch prediction job for all customers
Store predictions only as BigQuery materialized views
Use Vertex ML Metadata as the prediction service

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Deploy a model to an endpoint](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Deploy a model to an endpoint](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Deploy a model to an endpoint](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Deploy a model to an endpoint](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment).

questions.stem:
A marketing team needs scores for 30 million inactive accounts once per week, with results written to BigQuery.

Which Vertex AI feature is most appropriate?

options.text: Use endpoint traffic splitting between two models
Create a batch prediction job for the model
Enable online prediction autoscaling only
Run Explainable AI on a single request

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Get batch predictions from a model](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/predictions/get-batch-predictions).

**B** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Get batch predictions from a model](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/predictions/get-batch-predictions).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Get batch predictions from a model](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/predictions/get-batch-predictions).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Get batch predictions from a model](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/predictions/get-batch-predictions).

questions.stem:
A team wants to canary a new model version by sending 10% of online prediction traffic to it while 90% stays on the current model.

What should be configured?

options.text: A Dataflow side input with model version numbers
A BigQuery scheduled query over prediction logs
A Vertex AI endpoint traffic split across deployed models
A Feature Store batch serving export

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Traffic splitting for Vertex AI endpoints](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment#traffic-split).

**B** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Traffic splitting for Vertex AI endpoints](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment#traffic-split).

**C** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Traffic splitting for Vertex AI endpoints](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment#traffic-split).

**D** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Traffic splitting for Vertex AI endpoints](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/general/deployment#traffic-split).

questions.stem:
A recommendation system needs low-latency lookup of customer features at serving time and consistent batch export for training.

Which managed component is designed for this?

options.text: Cloud Logging sinks as the online feature database
Vertex AI Pipelines parameters only
Explainable AI sampled Shapley values
Vertex AI Feature Store for online and batch feature serving

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI Feature Store](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/featurestore).

**C** is incorrect because it changes a requirement in the scenario or applies a related service feature in a way that the documentation does not support for this use case. See [Vertex AI Feature Store](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/featurestore).

**D** is correct because this choice matches the documented behavior for the service in the scenario and satisfies the stated constraint without adding unnecessary components. See [Vertex AI Feature Store](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/featurestore).

questions.stem:
A training pipeline must parse Pub/Sub events, join them with reference data, and create features at scale before loading BigQuery.

Which service is best suited for this preprocessing flow?

options.text: Dataflow using Apache Beam transforms
Vertex AI endpoint traffic splitting
Cloud Build substitutions only
Model Monitoring skew thresholds

questions.explanation:
